pattern: A running prefix sum that repeats marks a zero-sum stretch, so storing the last node seen at each prefix value and relinking past repeats erases every such block in one pass.
hints: Think in prefix sums from a dummy head: whenever the running total revisits a value, every node strictly between those two points sums to zero and can be cut.
Keep the last node at which each prefix-sum value occurred; if a value recurs, splice the earlier node directly to the node after the later occurrence.
Do two passes over a dummy-headed list: first record final positions per prefix sum, then walk again linking each node to the saved successor of its prefix sum.

edge_inputs.description: leading block cancels to leave the tail
cascading cancellation collapses most of the list
whole list sums to zero, becomes empty

stress.description: moderate list over a small value range to create frequent prefix-sum repeats
stress.note: small magnitudes maximise zero-sum collisions; size kept low for the exponential validator

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def removeZeroSumSublists(self, head):
        dummy = ListNode(0)
        dummy.next = head
        last = {}
        s = 0
        node = dummy
        while node:
            s += node.val
            last[s] = node
            node = node.next
        s = 0
        node = dummy
        while node:
            s += node.val
            node.next = last[s].next
            node = node.next
        return dummy.next
